description: The irreducible kernel of the Theory of Liberty: what remains after removing all secondary material, illustrations, polemics, historical claims, and biographical content.

kernel_axioms.text: Human free will exists and is universally presupposed.
A valid formal system must have finite and minimal axioms.
kernel_axioms.why_kernel: Grounds the axiom-minimality argument and Tawhid necessity proof

kernel_axioms.text: A valid system must be internally consistent (Gödel).
kernel_axioms.why_kernel: Grounds the CFS methodology and religion=CFS claim

kernel_axioms.text: Tawhid: no servitude to anything other than God.
kernel_axioms.why_kernel: Gateway to theology, market, political theory
Required to solve last-round problem; cannot be derived secularly

kernel_axioms.text: Prophethood is real; it is the barrier against false messiahs.
kernel_axioms.why_kernel: Required to block false messiah problem; cannot be derived secularly

kernel_claims.text: Free will is the axiom everyone is forced to presuppose.
Denial of free will is self-refuting.
kernel_claims.why_kernel: Closes the free will argument against determinism

kernel_claims.text: Tawhid guarantees liberty by eliminating servitude to other-than-God.
Eliminating Tawhid proliferates axioms and leads to totalitarianism.
kernel_claims.why_kernel: The negative proof: removing Tawhid destroys liberty

kernel_claims.text: Liberty is unstable without Resurrection (last-round problem).
A consistent formal system is necessary for understanding true religion.
The formal system of liberty is equivalent to religion.
Liberty → property rights → religion (master derivation chain).
True religion purified of mysticism is the most precise system for liberty's defense.
Religion is the antithesis of the state.
Democracy does not legitimize violating individual rights.
Theory of liberty is the only existing solution for AI ethics.
Without Mahdism, the theory of liberty cannot be completed.

kernel_definitions.canonical: Liberty in its objective form is identical to individual property rights. It is the condition under which a person exercises sovereignty over their own body, labor, and acquired possessions, free from coercion by other human beings.

True religion is a consistent formal axiomatic system for individual liberty and property rights, purified of mysticism. It is not an institution, not a state-structure, and not a set of rituals. It is an axiomatic framework whose core axioms are Tawhid, Resurrection, and Prophethood.

Individual property rights are the exclusive authority of a person over their own body and legitimately acquired external goods. The Rule of Taslīṭ formalizes this: 'People are sovereign over their own property.' Property begins with the body.

Mysticism is any system — philosophical, religious, political, or cultural — that separates human beings from their body, reason, property, or individual identity, and delivers them to a collective entity (state, movement, mystical union, 'unity of being').

Tawhid is the principle that no human being has the right — in any way — to strip others of their power of choice and free will. Its negative definition: God is certainly not any of the humans or entities we see with our eyes. Its operational consequence: no servitude to anything other than God, which means no servitude to other humans.

Free will is the power of self-directed choice that every human being possesses and which is universally presupposed by all rational discourse, all contracts, and all moral claims. It is not derived; it is the necessary presupposition of derivation itself.

A consistent formal axiomatic system (CFS) is a system that is: (1) formally expressible in a language, (2) governed by logical rules, (3) whose definitions and concepts are objective (agreement-admissible), (4) axiomatic (derivation proceeds from a small set of unprovable but accepted premises), and (5) free of internal contradiction. Religion, purified, is such a system for liberty.

Resurrection (Ma'ad) functions in the theory as the game-theoretic solution to the last-round problem: because there is a final accounting whose timing is unknown, no agent can rationally defect in the 'last round.' This makes cooperation (liberty-respecting behavior) the dominant long-run strategy.

historical_claims: Iran as land of proprietors (C-000010, C-000060)
illustrative_examples: The unsupervised store / surveillance camera example
polemical_claims: Iranian communists mock classical liberals with 'invisible hand = Hand of God'
Yuval Noah Harari as example of godlike-human thinking
biographical_memoir: P-0183: Dey and Bahman 1402 arrest narrative
Personal reflections on the journey of writing

kernel_summary: The irreducible kernel consists of 6 axioms, 14 claims, and 8 definitions. Together they constitute the complete logical spine of the theory: Free Will → Property → Liberty → (Tawhid + Consistency) → Religion-as-CFS → Anti-Statism → AI-Ethics. The game-theoretic stability argument (Resurrection) and the anti-fraud argument (Prophethood) are required to close the secular-to-theological gap. All historical, civilizational, and illustrative material is in the protective belt, not the hard core.
